DE Wikipedia Audi_A6_C6 (facelift 2007-2011). 2.8 FSI up from 190 PS pre-facelift. 3.0 TFSI supercharged. CVT = multitronic; automatic = tiptronic. Wikidata ID covers full A6 model line; generation-specific ID not extracted.

DE Wikipedia Audi_A6_C7 (2011-2018). 1.8 TFSI added 09/2014 facelift; 3.0 TFSI supercharged dropped 2012. Hybrid = 2.0 TFSI + 40 kW EM, system 180 kW. DSG = 7-speed S-tronic. automatic = 8-speed tiptronic. 3.0 TDI 326 = biturbo. S6/RS6 excluded.

DE Wikipedia Audi_A6_C8 (2018-). All engines 48V mHEV. New numerical badge system: 35/40/45/50/55. DSG = 7-speed S-tronic; automatic = 8-speed tiptronic (PHEV). 55 TFSI e PHEV: ICE 195 kW + EM 105 kW ≈ 270 kW / 367 PS system. Diesel range ended ~2023. S6 TDI/RS6 excluded.

DE Wikipedia Audi_A6_C6 (pre-facelift 02/2004-08/2008). 2.0 TFSI from 04/2005. 2.8 FSI 209 PS from 10/2006 (replaced 190 PS 2.4 V6). 4.2 FSI quattro continues into facelift (year_to 2010). 3.0 TDI power bump 04/2006 (224→233 PS). CVT = multitronic; automatic = tiptronic. S6 (V10)/RS6 excluded.

DE Wikipedia Audi_Q5 (8R, 2008-2017). MLB platform. Facelift 2012. 3.2 FSI = CALA V6 NA (pre-FL only). 2.0 TFSI = EA888. 3.0 TFSI SC = CGWB. Q5 Hybrid: 2.0T + electric, non-plug, system 180kW. SQ5: diesel 230kW (2013-15) then petrol 3.0 TFSI SC 260kW (2015-17). S-tronic DSG = 7-speed dry / 8-speed wet. automatic = tiptronic.

DE Wikipedia Audi_Q5 (FY, 2017-2024). MLB evo platform. Facelift 2020. All 48V mHEV from 2020 facelift. 2.0 TFSI = EA888 gen4. SQ5 3.0 TFSI = EA839 mild hybrid. PHEV: 50 TFSIe 14.1kWh ~47km EV; 55 TFSIe 17.9kWh ~56km EV. 3.0 TDI = EA897. S-tronic 7-speed DSG. SQ5/3.0 TDI tiptronic automatic.

DE Wikipedia Audi_Q5 (GU, 09/2024-). PPC platform. All S-tronic 7-speed (DSG). 2.0 TFSI 204 from 09/2024; 272 from 11/2025. SQ5 3.0 TFSI from 09/2024. e-hybrid PHEV: two power tunes (220kW/299PS and 270kW/367PS), from 06/2025. 2.0 TDI 204 from 09/2024; 3.0 TDI 299 from 11/2025.

DE Wikipedia Audi_Q3 (8U, 2011-2018). PQ35 platform. LCI facelift 2014. 1.4 TFSI = EA111/EA211 from FL. RS Q3 = CEPA 2.5T 5-cyl: 228kW (2013), 250kW (2015), 270kW Performance (2016). 2.0 TDI = EA189/EA288. S-tronic DSG = 6-speed wet.

DE Wikipedia Audi_Q3 (F3, 2018-2025). MQB evo platform. 35 TFSI = EA211 1.5T ACT. 40/45 TFSI = EA888 2.0T. RS Q3 = DAZA 2.5T 5-cyl 294kW (2019-). 45 TFSIe PHEV: 1.4T + electric, system 180kW/245PS, 13kWh ~48km EV. 35/40 TDI = EA288 2.0. S-tronic DSG = 6/7-speed.

DE Wikipedia Audi_Q3 (FJ, 06/2025-). MQB evo2 platform. All S-tronic 7-speed DSG (e-hybrid 6-speed). 1.5 TFSI EA211 evo; 2.0 TFSI EA888 gen4. 1.5 TFSI e-hybrid: system 200kW/272PS, PHEV. 2.0 TDI EA288 evo. Euro 6e-bis.
DE Wikipedia Audi_Q2 (GA, 2016-). MQB A1 platform. Facelift 2020: 1.0 TFSI → 1.5 TFSI; 1.6 TDI → 2.0 TDI 116PS. SQ2: 2.0 TFSI quattro 300PS. DSG = 7-speed S tronic.
DE Wikipedia Audi_A1 (8X, 2010-2018). PQ25 platform. 1.4 TFSI cylinder-on-demand (CoD) versions: 140PS (2010) and 185PS (2014 S1-Linie). S1: EA888 quattro AWD.
DE Wikipedia Audi_A1 (GB, 2018-). MQB A0 platform. 25 TFSI = 1.0T 3-cyl 70kW; 30 TFSI = 1.0T 85kW; 35 TFSI = 1.5T 110kW. S1 discontinued for GB gen. DSG = 7-speed S tronic.
DE Wikipedia Audi_A3_8L (1996-2003). PQ34 platform. Golf IV sibling. AEB/AGN/APP/ARZ petrol, AGR/AHF/ASZ diesel. S3 = quattro AWD. Facelift 1999. Very popular as used premium compact in PL.

DE Wikipedia Audi_A4_B5 (8D, 1994-2001). Passat B5 sibling. ADR/APT petrol, AEB/ANB 1.8T, AFC/AGA V6, BBG 2.8. 1.9 TDI = AFN/AHH PD. S4 = AGB biturbo quattro. Facelift 1999. Extremely popular used import in PL 1998-2006.
DE Wikipedia Audi_A4_B6 (8E/8H, 2000-2004). New PL46 platform. 1.8T = BFB/AVJ. 2.0 FSI = BGB direct injection debut. 1.9 TDI = AWX/AVF pumpe-düse. Popular in PL 2003-2008 as used.
DE Wikipedia Audi_A4_B7 (8E/8H, 2004-2008). Facelift of B6 platform. 2.0T = BWT/BWE TFSI. 2.0 TDI = BPW/BRD. S4 = BBK 4.2 FSI V8. S-tronic DSG available. Popular in PL 2006-2012 as used.
DE Wikipedia Audi_A6_C5 (4B, 1997-2005). Passat B5 extended platform. AEB 1.8T, AGA/BDX V6. AYK 2.7T biturbo. BCE/BKH 4.2 V8 (S6). AFB/AKN 2.5 TDI V6. Facelift 2001. Popular E-segment import in PL.
DE Wikipedia Audi_80_B4 (8C, 1991-1994). D-segment. Short run before A4 debut. ABD/ABK/AAH V6 petrol, 1Z TDI diesel. S2 = 3B/AAN turbo 5-cyl quattro. Cabriolet continued to 2000. Popular used in PL.
DE Wikipedia Ford_Focus section 21/22. Covers full Mk2/'05 gen incl. 2008 facelift. Flexifuel/RS500 skipped.
DE Wikipedia Ford_Focus section 33/34. Ford PowerShift DCT mapped to DSG.
DE Wikipedia Ford_Focus section 39/40. 1.0 EcoBoost Hybrid = mild hybrid (48V). Focus discontinued 11/2025.

DE Wikipedia Ford_Fiesta_'09 section. Pre-facelift 2008-2012, facelift 2012-2017. DSG = 6-speed PowerShift. ST rated at 182 PS (147 kW overboost to 200 PS); ST200 rated at 200 PS (215 overboost). 1.0 NA 65/80 PS = naturally aspirated Ti-VCT.

DE Wikipedia Ford_Fiesta_'18 section. EU launch 2017. 1.1 NA 3-cylinder replaces 1.25. 1.0 EcoBoost 85 PS dropped after 2019 facelift. 1.5 EcoBoost ST replaces 1.6 unit. automatic = 7-speed DCT on 100 PS variant.

DE Wikipedia Ford_Kuga (Mk1 02/2008-10/2012). EU-only model (US = Escape). 2.5 T5 petrol = Volvo-derived. TDCi 136 PS replaced by 140/163 PS from 02/2010 facelift. DSG = 6-speed PowerShift. 4WD optional. Produced in Saarlouis. Wikidata ID covers full Kuga model line.

DE Wikipedia Ford_Kuga (Mk2 11/2012-06/2019). 1.6 EcoBoost replaced by 1.5 EcoBoost from 2016 facelift (150 PS dropped). 2.0 EcoBoost 242 PS added 11/2017. DSG = 6-speed PowerShift. 4x4 standard on top petrol/diesel variants.

DE Wikipedia Ford_Kuga (Mk3 2019-, production from 04/2020). FHEV = 2.5 Atkinson + 95 kW EM, system ~150 kW/190 PS, discontinued 2024. PHEV = 14.4 kWh, 56 km WLTP EV range, system 165 kW/225 PS. All diesel discontinued 02/2022. automatic = 8-speed. CVT = Ford e-CVT (Atkinson hybrid).

EN Wikipedia Ford_Mondeo (Mk4 BA7 pre-facelift, 2007-2010). EUCD platform. 2.3 Duratec = Mazda sourced. 2.5T = Volvo B5254T3 inline-5. 1.8 TDCi = DLD-418 PSA. 2.0 TDCi = PSA DW10. 2.2 TDCi = PSA DW12 twin-turbo 129kW. No PowerShift pre-facelift; automatic = 6-speed Aisin/SelectShift.

EN Wikipedia Ford_Mondeo (Mk4 BA7 facelift, 2010-2014). 1.6 EcoBoost = Dragon/Fox 1.6T turbocharged (new at FL). 2.0 EcoBoost from Ford EcoBoost family. 1.8 TDCi dropped; 2.3/2.5 dropped at FL. 2.2 TDCi uprated to 147kW/200PS. PowerShift = 6-speed DCT = DSG.

EN Wikipedia Ford_Mondeo (Mk5 CD391, 2014-2022). C2 platform. 2.0 HEV = 2.0 Atkinson + EM, system 138kW/187PS, eCVT. 1.5 EcoBlue from 2016 (EU6). 2.0 TDCi = Duratorq; Bi-TDCi biturbo for ST. HEV sedan from launch, estate from 2019. PowerShift DCT = DSG. automatic = 8-speed Aisin on Mk5.
DE Wikipedia Ford_Puma (JX, 2019-). C2 platform. mHEV = 48V mild hybrid → PB_95. 7DCT for auto variants. ST: 1.5T 3-cyl 200PS 6MT.
DE Wikipedia Ford_Galaxy (WA6, 2006-2015). CD3 platform, sibling of S-Max Mk1. PowerShift 6DCT → DSG. 2.0 TDCi DSG = 6AT PowerShift. 1.6/2.0 EcoBoost from facelift 2011.
DE Wikipedia Ford_Galaxy (Mk3/CK, 2015-). CD4 platform, sibling of S-Max Mk2. 2.5 FHEV = full hybrid non-plug e-CVT → automatic. DSG = 8AT SelectShift.
DE Wikipedia Ford_S-MAX (Mk1/WA6, 2006-2015). CD3 platform, sibling of Galaxy Mk2. 2.0 EcoBoost 240PS = ST-Line trim. PowerShift 6DCT → DSG.
DE Wikipedia Ford_S-MAX (Mk2/CJ, 2015-). CD4 platform, sibling of Galaxy Mk3. No PHEV on S-Max (Galaxy Mk3 exclusive). DSG = 8AT SelectShift.
DE Wikipedia Ford_C-MAX (Mk2/C344, 2010-2019). C1/CD3 platform. Includes Grand C-Max (longer wheelbase). 1.0 EcoBoost 3-cyl from 2012. DSG = PowerShift 6DCT.

DE Wikipedia Ford_Mustang (S550, 2015-2023). First Mustang officially sold in EU/PL. 2018 facelift: 2.3 EB EU-spec 290PS, 5.0 V8 450PS, 10AT → automatic. GT500: Predator 5.2L flat-plane V8 + 7G-DCT Tremec → DSG.
DE Wikipedia Ford_Mustang (S650, 2023-). EU-spec 5.0 V8 ~450PS. Dark Horse: flat-plane 5.0 V8 ~500PS with 6MT or optional Tremec 7DCT → DSG. Exact EU PS figures may vary slightly.
DE Wikipedia Ford_Expedition (U553, 2018-). Full-size BOF SUV. 3.5 EB twin-turbo V6; std 375HP/380PS; HO trim 400HP/406PS. 10AT SelectShift. RWD or 4WD. EU gray import. MAX = extended wheelbase.
DE Wikipedia Ford_Focus (DAW/DBW/DFW, 1998-2004). C-segment. Revolutionized handling in class. 1.8 TDDi = indirect injection; TDCi = common rail from 2001. RS = limited turbocharged version.
DE Wikipedia Ford_Galaxy_I (WGR, 1995-2006). Shared platform with VW Sharan / Seat Alhambra. 1.9 TDI = VAG engines. Very popular in PL as used family MPV 2000-2006.
DE Wikipedia Ford_Mondeo_III (B5Y/BWY/B4Y, 2000-2007). D-segment. CDHA/LCBA petrol. Duratec (HE) 2.0. ST220 = 3.0 Duratec. 2.0 TDCi = Duratorq DOHC. Popular in PL 2000-2007.
DE Wikipedia Ford_Fiesta_Mk3 (GFJ, 1989-1995). B-segment. HCS/CVH engines. XR2i = CVH 1.6 EFI. 1.8 D = Endura-D. Popular used import to PL early 1990s.
DE Wikipedia Ford_Fiesta_Mk4/5 (JH/JD, 1995-2002). Zetec-SE 1.25/1.4/1.6. Endura-D diesel. Facelift 1999 = Mk5 in some markets. Popular in PL 1997-2004 as new and used.
DE Wikipedia Ford_Escort_Mk5/6 (AAL/AFL/ALL, 1990-2000). C-segment. HCS/CVH/Zetec petrol, Endura-D/Endura-DT diesel. RS2000 = RWD. Facelift 1992 = Mk6. Common used import in PL 1993-2001.
DE Wikipedia Ford_Mondeo_I (GBP/BNP/BFP, 1993-1996). Car of the Year 1994. D-segment. Zeta/Duratec petrol, Endura-DT diesel. Sedan/hatch/estate. Benchmark D-segment in PL 1994-1999.
DE Wikipedia Ford_Mondeo_II (BAP/BFP/BNP, 1996-2000). Facelift of Mk1. Duratec 16v, Endura-DT diesel. ST200 = sport pack 2.5 V6. Very common in PL 1997-2003 as used D-segment.
DE Wikipedia BMW_E90. Covers E90/E91/E92/E93 family. 323i had two power outputs (N52B25). M3 = V8 4.0L.
DE Wikipedia BMW_F30 sections 33/38. M DCT mapped to DSG. 330e = PHEV system output.
DE Wikipedia BMW_G20. 318d/320d got 48V mild hybrid from 02/2020. PHEV system output. M DCT mapped to DSG.

DE Wikipedia BMW_5er_F10 (01/2010-01/2017). 520i/528i use N20 4-cyl (from 2011); earlier 528i was N53 6-cyl 258PS. 520d: N47 engine to 2014 (184PS), then B47 (190PS). 525d = N47 biturbo 4-cyl. 535i = N55 6-cyl. ActiveHybrid 5 = N55 + 40kW EM. automatic = 8-speed ZF. Wikidata ID covers full 5 Series model line; F10-specific ID not extracted.

DE Wikipedia BMW_5er_G30 (02/2017-08/2023). All B-series modular engines. 530i = B48 4-cyl; 540i/545e = B58 6-cyl. 530e PHEV: ICE 135 kW + EM 83 kW = 185 kW system. 545e: ICE 210 kW + EM 80 kW = 290 kW system. 520d manual to summer 2018 only. 525d discontinued 2019. LCI facelift 07/2020.

DE Wikipedia BMW_5er_G60 (launch 10/2023). All ICE = 48V mild hybrid. 530e PHEV: ICE 140 kW + EM 135 kW = 220 kW system. 550e: ICE 230 kW + EM 145 kW = 360 kW system. i5 shares G60 platform. 530i/540i petrol not available in EU (US/Asia only). automatic = 8-speed ZF. M5 PHEV (727PS) excluded.

DE Wikipedia BMW_X3 (F25, 2010-2017). LCI facelift 2014. 20i/28i = N20 2.0T 4-cyl; 35i = N55 3.0T 6-cyl. 20d = N47 2.0 pre-LCI (135kW), B47 2.0 LCI (140kW). 30d = N57 3.0 6-cyl biturbo. Manual 6-speed on 20i/20d pre-LCI; all post-LCI automatic only. automatic = ZF 8HP.

DE Wikipedia BMW_X3 (G01, 2017-2024). CLAR platform. LCI 2021. 20i/30i = B48 2.0T 4-cyl. M40i = B58 3.0T 6-cyl 48V mHEV. 20d = B47 2.0 4-cyl. 30d pre-LCI = B57 3.0 N57 biturbo 195kW; LCI = B57 210kW 48V mHEV. xDrive30e PHEV from LCI: 12kWh, ~50km EV. All automatic ZF 8HP.

DE Wikipedia BMW_X3 (G45, 2024-). CLAR evo platform. All engines 48V ISG mild hybrid. 20/30 = B48 2.0T 4-cyl. M50 = B58 3.0T 6-cyl. 20d = B47 2.0 4-cyl. 30d (B57 3.0 6-cyl) from 2025. xDrive30e PHEV: 19.5kWh, ~90km EV. All automatic ZF 8HP.

DE Wikipedia BMW_3er_E46 (1997-2006). Compact/saloon/touring/cabrio/coupe. M43/N42 (316i), M52/M54 (320-330), S54 (M3). M47 diesel (318d/320d), M57 (330d). SMG optional on M3 = DSG convention. Most popular used premium import in PL 2000-2010.
DE Wikipedia BMW_5er_E39 (1995-2003, estate to 2004). M52/M54 inline-6 petrol. M62 V8 (540i). S62 V8 (M5). M47 (520d) / M57 (525d/530d) diesel. Very popular as used luxury import in PL 2000-2008.

DE Wikipedia BMW_5er_E60 (2003-2010). Chris Bangle design. M54/N52 inline-6. N62 V8 (545i). S85 V10 (M5). N47 (520d) / M57N2 (525d/530d). SMG replaced by DKG (DSG) on M5. Popular in PL 2006-2012 as used E-segment.
DE Wikipedia BMW_3er_E36 (1990-2000). M43/M50/M52 petrol, S50/S52 (M3). M41 (318tds), M51 (325td/tds) diesel. Coupe/sedan/touring/convertible/compact. The defining 1990s used premium in PL.
DE Wikipedia BMW_5er_E34 (1988-1996). M43/M50/M60 petrol, S38 (M5), M51 diesel. Sedan/touring. First E34 Touring 1992. Very popular as used prestige import in PL 1995-2003.
DE Wikipedia Hyundai_Tucson sections 6/7. 1.6 CRDi from 2018 facelift; 1.7 CRDi pre-facelift. DSG = 7-speed DCT.
DE Wikipedia Hyundai_Tucson sections 10/11/12. 48V mild-hybrid petrol variants share power badges. HEV/PHEV = system output. iMT mapped to manual.
DE Wikipedia Hyundai_i30_(FD). 2.0 CVVT and 2.0 CRDi dropped 2010 (pre-Euro 5). 1.6 CRDi 128 PS not available in DE — skipped. automatic = 4-speed.
DE Wikipedia Hyundai_i30_(GD). automatic = 6-step; DSG = 7-speed DCT (from 04/2015 for GDI/CRDi 110). 1.4 blue = updated 1368 cc Gamma. 1.6 GDI Turbo = i30 Turbo Sport trim.

DE Wikipedia Hyundai_i30_(PD). DSG = 7-speed DCT (8-speed DCT for N Performance 280 optional). 1.5 DPI = naturally aspirated 1.5. Diesel range ended 01/2022. N variants production gap 04/2019–05/2021.
DE Wikipedia Hyundai_i30_(PD) section 06/2024+ (CN7 not yet a separate DE Wikipedia article). No diesel. 1.0 T-GDI replaces 1.0 120 PS; 1.5 T-GDI 140 PS replaces 160 PS as main powertrain.

DE Wikipedia Hyundai_Kona (OS, 2017-2023). 1.0 T-GDi (88kW) launch, replaced by 1.6 T-GDi. HEV: Theta II 1.6 GDi (104kW) + 32kW e-motor, 6DCT. Electric: 39.2kWh/100kW or 64kWh/150kW. Kona N: 2.0T 8DCT 206kW (2021-). Diesel 1.6 CRDi discontinued 2021.
DE Wikipedia Hyundai_Kona (SX2, 2023-). HEV: 1.6 GDi + 6MT (129PS) or 7DCT (141PS). EV: 48.4kWh/99kW or 65.4kWh/160kW. 1.6 T-GDi 7DCT = Kona performance variant.
DE Wikipedia Hyundai_Bayon (BC3, 2021-2025). B-SUV on i20 platform. 1.0 T-GDi in 3 power levels; 48V mHEV variant → PB_95. Production ended Nov 2025 per DE Wikipedia.
DE Wikipedia Hyundai_i20 (GB, 2014-2020). 1.0 T-GDi turbo from 2015 with 7DCT. 1.4 MPI with optional 4AT. Diesel: 1.1 CRDi 3-cyl and 1.4 CRDi 4-cyl.
DE Wikipedia Hyundai_i20 (BC3, 2020-). 1.0 T-GDi in 3 power tunes; from 12/2025 replaced by 48V mHEV variant → PB_95. i20 N: 1.6T 150kW 6MT performance variant.
DE Wikipedia Hyundai_i10 (IA, 2013-2019). 1.0 MPI 3-cyl and 1.2 MPI 4-cyl; both with optional 4AT. LPG variant existed but niche.
DE Wikipedia Hyundai_i10 (AC3, 2019-2025). Production ended Nov 2025. 1.0 T-GDi 100PS added 06/2020, dropped 05/2024. No automatic available on AC3 in EU from launch.
DE Wikipedia Hyundai_Elantra_XD (2000-2006). C/D-segment. G4ED (1.6) / G4GC (2.0) Theta engines. D4EA diesel. Sold in PL as affordable Korean alternative to VW/Ford.
DE Wikipedia Kia_Sportage SL section. 2.0 CVVT 150 KM (Mexico/Russia) skipped. AWD standard on 2.0 CRDi 184.
DE Wikipedia Kia_Sportage QL section. 1.6 CRDi from 2018 facelift replacing 1.7 CRDi. DSG = 7-speed DCT.
DE Wikipedia Kia_Sportage NQ5 section. 48V mild-hybrid shares power badges with regular T-GDI. PHEV system power; 3 variants across model years. HEV = 6-AT.

DE Wikipedia Kia_cee'd (JD, 04/2012-08/2018). 1.0 T-GDI and 1.6 CRDi 136 added at 06/2015 facelift. DSG = 6-speed DCT (petrol GT, CRDi 136). automatic = 6-speed torque converter (CRDi 110). Wikidata ID covers full cee'd/Ceed model line; generation-specific ID not found in DE article.

DE Wikipedia Kia_Ceed (CD, 06/2018-). 1.4 CVVT brief launch only (→ 1.4 T-GDI). 1.5 T-GDI replaces 1.4 T-GDI from 2021 facelift. HEV = 77 kW ICE + 44 kW EM, system 104 kW, hatchback only. PHEV = same system + 9.3–11.2 kWh battery, SW wagon only. New CRDi 1598 cc (U2 diesel) vs JD's 1582 cc. DSG = 6- or 7-speed DCT.

DE Wikipedia Kia_K4. EU launch 11/2025 (hatchback); SW Sportswagon 01/2026. Replaces Kia Ceed (CD). 1.0 T-GDI also available as 48V mHEV at same 85 kW — merged into one entry. 1.6 T-GDI offered in 150 PS and 180 PS tunes. No diesel or PHEV in EU lineup. NA variants excluded. DSG = 7-speed DCT.
DE Wikipedia Kia_Niro (DE, 2016-2022). HEV: Theta II 1.6 GDi 77kW + 32kW e-motor, 6DCT, system 104kW/141PS, non-plug → PB_95. PHEV: 45kW e-motor, 6DCT. EV: 39.2kWh/100kW or 64kWh/150kW.
DE Wikipedia Kia_Niro (SG2, 2022-). HEV: system 104kW/141PS, 6DCT non-plug → PB_95. PHEV: upgraded 62kW e-motor, system 135kW/183PS, 6DCT, 65km EV range. EV: 64.8kWh/150kW.
DE Wikipedia Kia_Stonic (YB, 2017-). Facelift Aug 2020 (engine update), second update May 2024 (diesel dropped, 120PS petrol added). Optional 7DCT on 1.0 T-GDi.
DE Wikipedia Kia_Ceed (ED, 2006-2012). C-segment. First Kia designed in Europe (Frankfurt). SW estate variant too. 1.6 CRDi = U2 engine. Facelift 2009 = pro_cee'd added.
DE Wikipedia Renault_Clio III facelift section. Only facelift-era verified engines included; base 1.2 16V/1.4 16V carry-overs not in DE section.
DE Wikipedia Renault_Clio IV section. DSG = 6-speed EDC dual-clutch. 0.9 TCe = 3-cylinder turbo.
DE Wikipedia Renault_Clio V section. E-Tech = full HEV, system power, DHT mapped to automatic. DSG = 7-speed EDC. 1.3 TCe 67/72 KM are detuned fleet variants.
DE Wikipedia Renault_Megane III section. 2.0 TCe 190 KM (brief 2012-13) merged into 180/220 range. DSG = 6-speed EDC. 2.0 16V uses CVT.
DE Wikipedia Renault_Megane IV section. DSG = 7-speed EDC. E-Tech PHEV system output. 1.6 dCi 165 badge = 163 PS actual.
DE Wikipedia Renault_Megane E-Tech section. EV40 = 40 kWh/300 km WLTP; EV60 = 60 kWh/450-470 km WLTP. All FWD. displacement_cc=0 (no ICE).

EN/FR Wikipedia Renault_Captur (Gen I J87, 2013-2019). B0 platform. TCe 90 = H4Bt 3-cyl. TCe 120 = H4Jt 4-cyl. TCe 130 = H5Ht 1.3T from 2017 facelift. dCi = K9K 1.5 diesel. EDC = 6-speed dual-clutch, labeled DSG.

EN/FR Wikipedia Renault_Captur (Gen II XJB, 2019-). CMF-B platform. TCe 90 = H4Dt 1.0T 3-cyl. TCe 140 = H5Ht 1.3T 4-cyl. E-Tech Hybrid: 1.6 Atkinson + 2 electric motors, system 105kW/145PS, no plug. E-Tech PHEV: system 118kW/160PS, 9.8kWh, ~45km EV. Blue dCi = K9K EU6d, phased out ~2023. EDC = DSG.

DE Wikipedia Renault_Scénic (JZ, 2009-2016). C platform. Facelift 2012. 1.4 TCe = H4Jt. 1.2 TCe Energy = H5Ft 3-cyl turbo (FL). 1.5 dCi = K9K. 1.9 dCi = F9Q. 1.6 dCi = R9M (FL). 2.0 dCi = M9R. automatic = 6-speed Aisin on 2.0 dCi.

DE Wikipedia Renault_Scénic (JFA, 2016-2022). CMF-C platform. TCe 115 = H5Ht 1.2T 3-cyl. TCe 140 = H5H 1.3T 4-cyl with EDC (DSG). 2018 refresh: old dCi replaced by Blue dCi. Blue dCi 120 = R9M 1.7L; Blue dCi 150 = M9R 2.0L; both with 6-speed EDC automatic.

DE Wikipedia Renault_Scénic E-Tech (2024-). CMF-EV platform (Renault-Nissan Alliance BEV). E-Tech 170: 60kWh, ~430km WLTP. E-Tech 218: 87kWh, ~625km WLTP. DC charging up to 150kW, AC 22kW. Pure EV — no ICE or PHEV variant.

DE Wikipedia Renault_Arkana (I, EU launch 2021). CMF-B platform. TCe 140: 1.3T EDC7 wet DCT → DSG. E-Tech: 1.6L + 2 e-motors, dog-clutch multi-mode → automatic. Facelift 2024: TCe 140 dropped, E-Tech upgraded to 160PS.

DE Wikipedia Renault_Austral (I, 2022-). CMF-CD platform. TCe 130 with optional 6MT. TCe 160 7DCT → DSG. E-Tech Full Hybrid 200: 1.6L + 2 e-motors, dog-clutch DHT, non-plug → PB_95. PHEV 300: 22.5kWh plug-in.
DE Wikipedia Renault_Clio_II (BB/CB, 1998-2005). B-segment. Facelift 2001. K4J/K4M petrol, K9K diesel. RS 172/182 Sport trim. Very popular in PL 2000-2005.
DE Wikipedia Renault_Mégane_I (BA/DA/EA/KA, 1995-2002). C-segment. F9Q diesel, K4M/F4R petrol. Coupe/convertible/hatch variants. Sold until 2002 in PL.
DE Wikipedia Renault_Mégane_II (BM/CM, 2002-2008). C-segment. K9K diesel (1.5 dCi). RS 225/Trophy version popular in PL tuner scene. Facelift 2005.
DE Wikipedia Renault_Scénic_I (JA, 1996-2003). Compact MPV, based on Megane I. First modern compact MPV mainstream success in EU. Very popular in PL 1999-2003.
DE Wikipedia Renault_Scénic_II (JM, 2003-2009). Compact MPV. Grand Scénic = 7-seat version. K9K diesel popular. Facelift 2006. Strong seller in PL 2003-2009.
DE Wikipedia Renault_Clio_I (BB/CB, 1990-1998). Car of the Year 1991. B-segment. C3G/F3P/F7P petrol, F8Q diesel. Williams = F7R 2.0 150PS homologation special. Popular in PL 1993-2000 as used.
DE Wikipedia Renault_19 (B/C53, 1988-1996). C-segment, predecessor to Megane. E7J/F3P/F7P petrol, F8Q diesel. 16S = F7P 16v. Chamade = sedan variant. Used import in PL 1992-1998.
DE Wikipedia Renault_Laguna_I (B56, 1993-2001). D-segment. F7R/F8Q/G8T/Z7X petrol, F9Q/G9T diesel. Hatch only (5-door). Nevada estate also. Facelift 1998. Popular D-segment in PL 1996-2002.
DE Wikipedia Dacia_Duster I section. 4x2 and 4x4 share engine specs. dCi 86 very brief (03-09/2010). dCi 110 badge = 107-110 PS across production; 80 kW from 10/2013.
DE Wikipedia Dacia_Duster II section. DSG = 6-speed EDC. ECO-G = bifuel LPG/petrol. 1.2 TCe 125 and 1.6 SCe very brief (01-09/2018 transition). 1.3 TCe 130 badge = 131 PS actual.
DE Wikipedia Dacia_Duster III section. No diesel. ECO-G = LPG/petrol bifuel. Mild Hybrid = 48V starter-generator. Full Hybrid = system power. Hybrid-G = LPG + hybrid AWD.

DE Wikipedia Dacia_Sandero (Gen II B52, 2012-2020). Facelift 2016. 0.9 TCe = H4Bt 3-cyl turbo. 1.2 16V = D4F pre-facelift only. 1.0 SCe = H4D naturally aspirated (facelift). 0.9 TCe Bifuel = LPG+petrol dual-fuel system. 1.5 dCi = K9K; 95 PS from 2018 (EU6c). No automatic on diesel. Easy-R automated-manual offered but negligible volume.

DE Wikipedia Dacia_Sandero (Gen III BJI, 2020-). CMF-B platform. No diesel. 1.0 SCe = naturally aspirated. 1.0 TCe = turbocharged (H4D-H series). ECO-G = bifuel LPG+petrol. CVT = X-Tronic on 1.0 TCe 90. 1.0 TCe 100 PS petrol (non-ECO-G) from 2022 facelift. Facelift 2022 added mild cosmetic updates.

DE Wikipedia Dacia_Jogger (2021-). Sales from 03/2022. No diesel. ECO-G = factory petrol/LPG bifuel; LPG-mode peak power listed. E-Tech 140: 1.6L + 51kW e-motor, 8-speed DHT multi-mode, non-plug HEV → PB_95. Hybrid 155: 1.8L from 12/2025.

DE Wikipedia Mercedes-Benz_Baureihe_204. C 180 uses M271/M274 (1597-1595 cc); 1595 cc used as final spec. C 200/C 250 use M271 1796 cc. automatic = 5G/7G-Tronic. C 300 covers C 280 (2007-09) and C 300 (2009-11). C 350 CDI 231 covers C 320 CDI (2007-09) and C 300/350 CDI (2009-14). 4MATIC available on C 220/250/350 CDI.

DE Wikipedia Mercedes-Benz_Baureihe_205. C 180/200 post-07/2018 use M264 1497 cc with EQ Boost (mHEV); badge power unchanged. C 200 d and C 220 d engines swapped to OM654 (1597/1950 cc) from 07/2018 facelift. PHEV = system power. automatic = 7G/9G-Tronic. 4MATIC available on C 200/300/220d/250d/300d.

DE Wikipedia Mercedes-Benz_Baureihe_206. All petrol and diesel are 48V EQ Boost mHEV (ICE + 17 kW e-motor); power listed = ICE only (badge). All 9G-Tronic. C 200 4MATIC shares spec with C 200 RWD. AMG C 63 S E Performance (680 PS PHEV, 2023-) omitted as extreme outlier.
DE Wikipedia Mercedes-Benz_A-Klasse (W176, 2012-2018). MFA platform. 7G-DCT = 7-speed dual clutch → DSG. 1.6T (160/180), 2.0T (200/250). A 45 AMG = M133 high-output 2.0T.
DE Wikipedia Mercedes-Benz_A-Klasse (W177, 2018-). MFA2 platform. 1.3T = M282 (Renault-sourced); 2.0T = M260. 8G-DCT → DSG. A 250 e: 1.3T + 75kW e-motor, 15.6kWh PHEV.
DE Wikipedia Mercedes-Benz_GLC (X253, 2015-2023). C-class platform. 9G-Tronic torque converter → DSG convention. PHEV: 2.0T + e-motor, 13.5kWh.
DE Wikipedia Mercedes-Benz_GLC (X254, 2022-). New platform, 48V mild hybrid standard. GLC 200 = 1.5T M254 + 48V ISG. PHEVs: 31.2kWh large battery, ~120km EV range. DSG = 9G-Tronic.
DE Wikipedia Mercedes-Benz_GLB (X247, 2019-). MFA2 platform. 7-seat compact SUV. 1.3T M282 (Renault) or 2.0T M260. 8G-DCT/9G-Tronic → DSG. PHEV: 15.6kWh.
DE Wikipedia Mercedes-Benz_CLA (C117, 2013-2019). MFA platform. 7G-DCT → DSG. Sedan-coupé, 4-door.
DE Wikipedia Mercedes-Benz_CLA (C118, 2019-). MFA2 platform. 1.3T M282 or 2.0T M260. 8G-DCT/7G-DCT → DSG. PHEV: 15.6kWh.
DE Wikipedia Mercedes-Benz_GLE (V167, 2018-). GLE 450/580/53 AMG = 48V EQ Boost mHEV (ISG) → PB_95. GLE 350 de: 2.0d + e-motor plug-in, 31.2kWh. 9G-Tronic → DSG.

DE Wikipedia Mercedes-Benz_C-Klasse_W203 (2000-2007). D-segment. M271 supercharged (Kompressor). M111 older units. OM611/OM646 CDI diesel. Facelift 2004. Sportcoupe / Estate also. Popular used premium in PL.

DE Wikipedia Mercedes-Benz_E-Klasse_W211 (2002-2009). E-segment. M271 (E200K), M272 V6, M113 V8 (E55). OM646 (220 CDI), OM648 (270 CDI), OM642 V6 (280/320 CDI). Facelift 2006. Very popular used E-segment in PL.

DE Wikipedia Mercedes-Benz_C-Klasse_W202 (1993-2000). D-segment. M111/M103/M104/M113 petrol. OM601/OM604/OM605/OM646 diesel. Sedan/estate/coupe. CDI from 1999. Defining entry-premium used car in PL 1996-2005.

DE Wikipedia Mercedes-Benz_E-Klasse_W210 (1995-2002). E-segment. M111/M104/M112/M113 petrol. OM604/OM605/OM611/OM612/OM613 diesel. CDI common rail from 1999. Facelift 1999. Popular used E-segment in PL 2000-2008.

DE Wikipedia Nissan_Qashqai (J10 facelift 03/2010-11/2013). 1.6 petrol up from 114 PS. 1.6 dCi added 2011. 2.0 engines available with 4WD option. CVT = X-TRONIC; DSG = auto on diesel. Wikidata covers full J10 generation.

DE Wikipedia Nissan_Qashqai (J11 2013-2021). 1.2 DIG-T dropped 08/2018; replaced by 1.3 DIG-T (co-developed with Renault). 1.6 dCi replaced by 1.7 dCi (R9N) from 2019. 1.3 DIG-T 160 PS = late J11 pre-J12 bridge variant. DSG = 7-speed DCT (petrol) or auto (diesel). 4WD available on 1.3 140/1.6 dCi/1.7 dCi.

DE Wikipedia Nissan_Qashqai (J12 06/2021-). No diesel. All petrol = 12V mHEV. e-Power = serial hybrid: 1.5 VC-T (variable compression) ICE acts as generator only; 140 kW electric motor drives wheels; listed power = motor output. CVT = X-TRONIC e-CVT (e-Power) or X-TRONIC (mHEV). 4WD option on 1.3 160 and e-Power.
DE Wikipedia Nissan_Juke (F15, 2010-2019). Facelift 2014: 1.2 DIG-T replaces 1.6 NA. 1.6 DIG-T 190PS Nismo/4WD. CVT on 1.6 NA.
DE Wikipedia Nissan_Juke (F16, 2019-). CMF-B platform. 1.0 DIG-T 3-cyl turbo; DCT7 → DSG. HEV: 1.6 + e-motor, series/parallel, 4AT-like → automatic. No diesel in F16.
DE Wikipedia Nissan_Primera (P12, 2002-2008). D-segment. 2.0 CVT = xtronic. Controversial squared styling. Last Primera generation. Sold in PL as affordable D-segment.
DE Wikipedia Nissan_Almera_N16 (2000-2006). C/D border segment. QG15DE/QG18DE petrol. K9K (1.5 dCi) / YD22DDTi diesel. Popular in PL as affordable reliable sedan.

DE Wikipedia Volvo_XC60 (Mk1 Y20, 2008-2017). T5/T6 Drive-E (1969cc 4-cyl) from 2014 facelift; original T6 = B6324S 2953cc inline-6 supercharged. 3.2 = B6324S2 NA 6-cyl. D3/D4 = D5204 4-cyl; D5 = D5244 5-cyl biturbo (2400cc). automatic = Geartronic 6-speed (pre-fl) or 8-speed (post-fl).

DE Wikipedia Volvo_XC60 (Mk2 SPA, 2017-). All Drive-E 2.0T (1969cc). T→B rebadging 2020 with 48V mHEV integration (same power). T8 PHEV: ICE 228 kW + EM 107 kW rear axle, system 287 kW/390 PS; 14.9 kWh battery; ~50 km WLTP EV; Recharge branding from 09/2021. Diesel phased out ~2023. automatic = 8-speed Geartronic.

DE Wikipedia Volvo_XC40 (536, 2017-). CMA platform. B-series = 48V mHEV from 2020 → PB_95. T4 Recharge PHEV: 1.5T + e-motor, 7DCT. EV: 78kWh single/dual motor. Diesel D3/D4 dropped 2020. DSG = 8AT (Aisin) or 7DCT.

DE Wikipedia Volvo_XC90 (II, 2015-). SPA platform. T5 supercharged; T6 turbo+supercharged. T8 PHEV: 2.0T + twin e-axle rear motor; upgraded from 390PS to 455PS 2022. B5 diesel 48V mHEV. DSG = 8AT Aisin.
DE Wikipedia Volvo_V60 (II, 2018-). SPA platform. B-series = 48V mHEV from 2020 (both petrol and diesel). T6 Recharge PHEV: 2.0T + rear e-motor, 8AT. DSG = 8AT Aisin.
DE Wikipedia Volvo_V40 (525/526, 2012-2019). FWD only except V40 Cross Country AWD. Drive-E engines from 2014/2015 (1.5T T2/T3, 2.0 D-series). DSG = 8AT Geartronic (Aisin) or 6DCT.
DE Wikipedia Volvo_V50 (545, 2004-2012). Ford C1 platform. T5 = 2.5T 5-cyl. D5 = 2.4D 5-cyl; AWD optional. DSG = Geartronic 5AT or 6AT. Facelift 2007 added T4 2.0T.
DE Wikipedia Volvo_S60 (II/134, 2010-2018). SPA predecessor platform. Drive-E 2.0 engines from 2014. T6 = 2.0T turbo+supercharged. T8 PHEV: 2.0T + rear e-axle 11.2kWh. DSG = 8AT Geartronic.
DE Wikipedia Volvo_S60 (III/224, 2018-). SPA platform. B-series = 48V mHEV from 2020 → PB_95. T8 Recharge: 2.0T + rear e-axle, upgraded from 390PS to 455PS in 2022. No diesel in S60 III.
DE Wikipedia Volvo_S90 (II/234, 2016-). SPA platform, D-segment luxury. T6 = turbo+supercharged 2.0T. B6 = 48V ISG mHEV 221kW. T8: 2.0T + rear e-axle. Diesel dropped 2020. DSG = 8AT Geartronic.

DE Wikipedia VW_Polo (9N 2001-2005, 9N3 facelift 2005-2009). 1.2 = AZQ/BME 3-cyl. 1.4 16V = BBY/BKY. GTI = 9N3 only, 1.8T AWP/BUD. 1.9 SDI = 9N only. 1.4 TDI = AMF/BNM PD; 9N3 switched to CR. 1.9 TDI = ATD/BLT PD. automatic = 4-speed Tiptronic on petrol.

DE Wikipedia VW_Polo (6R pre-facelift, 2009-2014). PQ25 platform. 1.2 MPI = CGP 3-cyl. 1.2 TSI = CBZ twin-charged (supercharged+turbo). 1.4 TSI GTI = CAVE twin-charged 180 PS, DSG only (discontinued 2013). 1.2 TDI = CFWA 3-cyl BlueMotion. 1.4/1.6 TDI = CR. DSG = 7-speed dry.

DE Wikipedia VW_Polo (6C facelift, 2014-2017). 1.0 MPI/TSI = EA111 3-cyl replacing 1.2 units. 1.4 TSI BlueGT = EA211 with cylinder deactivation. 1.8 TSI GTI = EA888 replacing twin-charged 1.4 TSI. 1.4 TDI = EA288 3-cyl. DSG = 7-speed dry.

DE Wikipedia VW_Polo (AW, 2017-). MQB-A0 platform. 1.0 TSI = EA211 evo. 1.5 TSI ACT = EA211 evo with cylinder deactivation (from 2020 facelift). GTI: 147kW/200PS (2017-2021), 152kW/207PS (2022+ FL). 1.0 TGI = CNG+petrol bifuel. 1.6 TDI discontinued ~2020 (EU6d). DSG = 7-speed dry.

DE Wikipedia Peugeot_308 (T7, 2007-2013). PF2 platform. 1.4/1.6 VTi = EP3/EP6 DOHC NA. 1.6 THP = EP6DT turbo. GTI = EP6 DTS high-tune from 2010. 1.6 HDi = DV6 TDCI. 2.0 HDi = DW10 TDCI. automatic = AL4/EAT6.

DE Wikipedia Peugeot_308 (T9, 2013-2021). EMP2 platform. Facelift 2017. 1.2 PureTech = EB2 3-cyl turbo. 1.6 THP 205 GT = EP6 high-tune. 2017 FL replaced 1.6 HDi/BlueHDi with 1.5 BlueHDi (DV5). 2.0 BlueHDi = DW10. DSG = EAT6/EAT8. automatic = EAT6.

DE Wikipedia Peugeot_308 (P51, 2021-). EMP2 evolved platform. 1.2 PureTech = EB2 ADTED. 1.6 Hybrid 136 = 48V mild hybrid e-DSC6 dual-clutch. PHEV: 1.6 petrol + electric, 12.4kWh, ~60km EV (180PS) / 15.6kWh PHEV 225. e-308 = 54kWh, ~400km WLTP. automatic = EAT8. DSG covers EDC/e-DSC6/e-EAT8.
DE Wikipedia Peugeot_208 (A9, 2012-2019). PF1 platform. PureTech 3-cyl from facelift 2015. DSG = EAT6 6AT. GTi 200PS = 1.6 THP.
DE Wikipedia Peugeot_208 (UB/CMP, 2019-). CMP platform. DSG = EAT8 8AT. e-208: 50kWh/100kW, updated 54kWh/115kW from 2024.
DE Wikipedia Peugeot_2008 (A94, 2013-2019). 1.2 PureTech from 2013 (3-cyl). DSG = EAT6.
DE Wikipedia Peugeot_2008 (UB/CMP, 2019-). CMP platform. DSG = EAT8 8AT. e-2008: 50kWh/100kW, updated 54kWh/115kW from 2024.
DE Wikipedia Peugeot_3008 (I/facelift, EMP2, 2016-2024). PHEV: 1.6T + e-motor FWD 225PS or AWD 300PS (front 165kW ICE + rear e-axle). DSG = EAT8 8AT.
DE Wikipedia Peugeot_3008 (II, STLA Medium, 2024-). New platform. E-3008: 73kWh/157kW or AWD 235kW. 1.2 PureTech with 48V mHEV. PHEV: 21kWh plug-in.
DE Wikipedia Peugeot_5008 (I, EMP2, 2017-2024). 7-seat SUV. Same powertrain family as 3008 I but no PHEV. DSG = EAT6/EAT8.
DE Wikipedia Peugeot_508 (II, EMP2, 2018-). D-segment. PHEV: 1.6T + rear e-motor. 225PS FWD; 360PS AWD (Peugeot Sport Engineered). DSG = EAT8.
DE Wikipedia Peugeot_206 (2A/C/H, 1998-2010). B-segment. TU/TU5JP4 petrol, DW engines diesel. GTi 180 variant (not listed). RC = hot-hatch. Long production run until 2012 in some markets.
DE Wikipedia Peugeot_307 (3A/C, 2001-2007). C-segment. PF2 platform. Sedan/SW wagon/CC coupe variants. Facelift 2005. Popular in PL 2002-2007 especially diesel.
DE Wikipedia Peugeot_205 (20A/C, 1983-1998). Car of the Year 1984. B-segment. TU engine family. GTI 1.6/1.9 = legendary hot hatch. Very popular as used import in PL 1990-1998.
DE Wikipedia Peugeot_106 (1A/C, 1991-2003). A/B-segment. TU series engines. GTI 1.6 = TU5JP4 hot hatch. Facelift 1996. Electric version also. Popular budget import in PL 1993-2002.
DE Wikipedia Peugeot_306 (7A/C, 1993-2002). C-segment. TU/XU engines petrol, DW diesel. GTI-6 = XU10J4RS 167PS manual 6-speed. Cabrio variant. Popular used import in PL 1996-2003.
DE Wikipedia Peugeot_406 (8B/C, 1995-2004). D-segment. XU/EW petrol, DW diesel. Coupe by Pininfarina. HDi = common rail from 1999. Sedan/estate/coupe. Popular in PL 1998-2006 especially diesel.

DE Wikipedia Seat_Ibiza (6J, 2008-2017). PQ25 platform. Facelift 2012. 1.2 TSI = EA111 CBZ. 1.4 TSI FR/Cupra = EA111 CAVE twin-charged; Cupra 180 DSG only. 1.8 TSI Cupra = EA888 from 2015. 1.2 TDI = CFWA 3-cyl BlueMotion. 1.6 LPG = bifuel. 2.0 TDI FR = EA189. DSG = 7-speed dry.

DE Wikipedia Seat_Ibiza (KJ1, 2017-). MQB-A0 platform. 1.0 TSI = EA211 evo 3-cyl. 1.5 TSI = EA211 evo ACT cylinder deactivation. 1.0 TGI = CNG+petrol bifuel. 1.6 TDI discontinued 2019 (EU6d). No Cupra in Ibiza V generation (became standalone brand). DSG = 7-speed dry.
DE Wikipedia Seat_Leon III (5F, 2012-2020). MQB platform. 1.2 TSI → 1.5 TSI at facelift 2018. Cupra sub-brand: 265→290→300 PS upgrades 2014-2018, all Cupra treated as Seat pre-2018. TDI: EA288.

DE Wikipedia Seat_Leon IV (KL, 2020-). MQB evo platform. 1.0 eTSI and 1.5 eTSI = 48V mild hybrid → PB_95. PHEV 204PS: system 150kW, 6-speed DQ400E wet DSG, ICE 110kW. From 07/2024 facelift: eTSI replaces TSI, 1.5 eTSI 130PS. Cupra-specific variants listed under Cupra Leon.
DE Wikipedia Seat_Arona (KJ, 2017-). MQB A0 platform. Sibling of VW T-Cross. 1.0 e-TSI = 48V mHEV from facelift 2021 → PB_95. DSG = 7-speed.
DE Wikipedia Seat_Ateca (KH, 2016-). MQB A1 platform. Sibling of Skoda Karoq. Cupra Ateca listed separately under Cupra make. 1.6 TDI dropped at facelift 2020-2021. DSG = 7-speed.
DE Wikipedia Seat_Ibiza (6L, 2002-2008). PQ24 platform. FR = sporty trim. 1.8T = VAG AGR. TDI = PD pumpe-düse. Popular budget B-segment in PL.
DE Wikipedia Seat_León_1P (2005-2012). PQ35 platform. FR = sport trim. Cupra 265 = 2.0 TSI AWD optional. Facelift 2009. Popular in PL as sporty VW-group C-segment.
DE Wikipedia Seat_Ibiza_6K (1993-2002). PQ24 platform. AEH/AKQ/ABF/APE petrol, AGP/AGR diesel. GTI 2.0 = ABF. 3/5-door + Cordoba sedan. Facelift 1999. Popular in PL 1995-2004.

DE Wikipedia Mercedes-Benz_W212 (2009-2016). Facelift 2013: E 200/E 250 switched M271 1.8T→M274 2.0T (same power badges). E 300/E 350/E 400 = M276 3.0/3.5 V6. E 63 AMG = M157 5.5 V8 biturbo (from 2011; pre-fl M156 NA). Diesel = OM651 2.1T I4 / OM642 3.0 V6. automatic = 7G-Tronic.

DE Wikipedia Mercedes-Benz_W213 (2016-2023). Facelift 2020. E 180 = M264 1.6T (2017-2020). E 200/300 = M274 2.0T. E 450 = M276 3.0 V6 biturbo 48V mHEV. E 63 AMG = M177 4.0 V8 biturbo. Diesel = OM654 2.0T I4 (EU6). PHEV from FL 2020: E 300e system 235kW; E 300de system 225kW; both 13.5kWh ~50km EV. automatic = 9G-Tronic.

DE Wikipedia Mercedes-Benz_W214 (2023-). MRA2 platform. All engines 48V ISG mild hybrid standard. E 200/300 = M254 2.0T I4. E 450 = M256 3.0 I6. E 53 AMG PHEV = M256 3.0T + EQ booster, 25.4kWh, ~100km EV. Diesel = OM654 2.0T / OM656 3.0 I6. PHEV 25.4kWh ~100km EV. automatic = 9G-Tronic.

DE Wikipedia Fiat_500 (312, 2007-2024). Produced in Tychy, Poland. Facelifts 2015, 2020. 1.0 mHEV = FireFly 48V BSG (2020 FL). TwinAir = 2-cyl 875cc SGE turbo. TwinAir CNG = gas+petrol bifuel. Dualogic AMT = mapped to automatic. 1.3 Multijet = 55kW pre-2010, 70kW with DPF from 2009. Abarth automatic = Competizione sequential AMT. 500e BEV (332 platform) listed separately.

DE Wikipedia Fiat_Tipo (356, 2015-). Available as saloon, hatchback, estate. Facelift 2022: 1.4/1.6 MultiJet dropped; new 1.0 Firefly and 1.5 PureTech Hybrid (PSA sourced, 48V mHEV). DSG = 6AT on 1.4T; EAT8 on 1.5 Hybrid.
DE Wikipedia Fiat_Panda (169, 2003-2012). 4x4 variant with same petrol engines. CNG = factory bifuel (petrol/CNG).
DE Wikipedia Fiat_Panda (319, 2011-). 1.0 Hybrid = 12V mHEV (BSG), non-plug → PB_95. 1.0 CNG = factory bifuel. Long lifecycle, still produced. 1.2 Fire discontinued 2022.
DE Wikipedia Fiat_Punto (199, 2005-2018). Long lived model; production ended 2018, sold until stock cleared. Abarth Grande Punto: 1.4 T-Jet tuned.
DE Wikipedia Fiat_Seicento (187, 1998-2010). Successor to Cinquecento. A-segment. Very popular in PL as cheap city car and first car. Sporting 1.1 was a cult trim.
DE Wikipedia Fiat_Stilo (192, 2001-2007). C-segment. FIRE/Fire engines petrol. 1.9 JTD Multijet common rail diesel. 3-door/5-door/Multiwagon. Abarth = 2.4 5-cyl. Popular in PL early 2000s.
DE Wikipedia Fiat_126 'Maluch' (650, 1972-2000). Air-cooled 2-cyl rear engine. Produced in Bielsko-Biała PL. Most iconic Polish car ever. EL version with catalyst 1994+. Production ended 2000.
DE Wikipedia Fiat_Cinquecento (170, 1991-1998). A-segment successor to 126p. Produced in Tychy (Bielsko). 704cc/899cc 2-cyl. Sporting 1.1 = FIRE inline-4. Replaced by Seicento 1998.
DE Wikipedia Fiat_Uno (138, 1983-1995). B-segment hatch, Car of the Year 1984. FIRE engine family. Turbo ie = turbocharged 1.4. Very common in PL 1990s as first car after communism.
DE Wikipedia Fiat_Punto (176, 1993-1999). Car of the Year 1995. B-segment. FIRE 1.1/1.2, 1.4 Turbo GT. 1.7 D = indirect injection. Very popular in PL mid-1990s as new B-segment.

DE Wikipedia Tesla_Model_3. Mk1 (2017-2023). RWD variants: Long Range 75kWh 2017-2019; Mid Range ~62kWh 2018-2019; Standard Range 52kWh 2019; SR+ 58kWh 2019-2021. AWD/Performance pre-Highland specs (258kW/331kW) from EN Wikipedia / Tesla spec sheets — DE Wikipedia treats these as continuous lines without pre-Highland split, hence medium confidence.

DE Wikipedia Tesla_Model_3. Highland facelift (09/2023-). Standard RWD: LFP 60kWh 208kW/283PS, launched Nov 2021 pre-Highland, continues unchanged. LR RWD: NMC 79kWh 235kW/320PS, WLTP 702km. LR AWD: system 324kW/441PS (365kW/496PS with Acceleration Boost OTA). Performance AWD: 338kW/460PS, reintroduced Apr 2024 after brief Highland-launch hiatus.

DE Wikipedia Cupra_Formentor (2020-2024). MQB evo platform. 1.5 TSI = EA211 evo. 2.0 TSI = EA888. VZ5 2.5 TSI 5-cyl = EA855 evo, shared with Audi RS3, limited 7000 units. e-Hybrid PHEV: EA211 1.4T + 85kW e-motor, system power 150kW/204PS or 180kW/245PS, 6-speed DSG. 2.0 TDI = EA288 evo.

DE Wikipedia Cupra_Formentor facelift (2024-). EA211 evo 2 replaces EA211 evo. 1.5 eTSI = 48V mild hybrid. VZ5 2.5 TSI 5-cyl = EA855 evo, limited 4000 units, reintroduced 2025. e-Hybrid PHEV upgraded to 1.5T EA211 evo 2; VZ variant ICE 130kW/177PS + 85kW e-motor = system 200kW/272PS. TDI switches to 7DSG, AWD diesel discontinued.

DE Wikipedia Seat_Ateca/Cupra Ateca (KH, 2018-2024). MQB A1 platform. Launched as Seat Ateca Cupra 300PS (2018); became Cupra Ateca 2020. Entry variants 1.5T/2.0T 190PS added 06/2023. Production ended 04/2024. No diesel, no PHEV. All 7-speed DSG.

DE Wikipedia Cupra_Leon (KL, 2020-). MQB evo platform. 1.5 eTSI 48V mHEV → PB_95. 2.0 TSI 245PS FWD; 310PS AWD 4Drive Sportstourer. PHEV pre-facelift: 1.4T (110kW ICE + 85kW e-motor), 6-speed DQ400E wet DSG. Post-facelift (07/2024): upgraded to 1.5T engine, VZ 272PS system 200kW.
DE Wikipedia Mazda_CX-5 (KE, 2011-2017). SKYACTIV platform. 6AT = SKYACTIV-Drive torque converter. 2.2D with optional AWD.
DE Wikipedia Mazda_CX-5 (KF, 2017-). SKYACTIV-Drive 6AT torque converter. e-SKYACTIV G = 24V mHEV from 2022 → PB_95. 2.5T turbocharged petrol from 2019. Optional AWD on most variants.
DE Wikipedia Mazda_3 (BP, 2019-). SKYACTIV-X = SPCCI (spark-controlled compression ignition), 24V mHEV integrated. e-SKYACTIV G = 24V mHEV. SKYACTIV-Drive 6AT.
DE Wikipedia Mazda_CX-30 (DM, 2019-). e-SKYACTIV G 2.0 e-Drive = full HEV (series-parallel), system 150kW, non-plug → PB_95. SKYACTIV-Drive 6AT.
DE Wikipedia Mazda_CX-60 (KH, 2022-). New large platform, inline-6 diesel. PHEV: 2.5L Atkinson + e-motor, system 241kW plug-in. 8AT torque converter.
DE Wikipedia Mazda_323_BG (1989-1994). C-segment. B3/B6/BP engines. GTX = 4WD turbo rally homologation. Hatch/sedan. Used import in PL 1993-1999 as reliable C-segment alternative.
DE Wikipedia Mazda_323_BA (1994-1998). C-segment. B3/Z5/BP engines. Sedan/hatch/estate. Last 323 before rebrand to Mazda 3. Used in PL 1997-2003.
DE Wikipedia Mitsubishi_Outlander (III, GF, 2012-2022). PHEV: 2.4 Atkinson + front + rear e-motors, series-parallel; system 165kW, 13.8kWh plug-in. CVT = INVECS-III CVT. Diesel discontinued 2021.
DE Wikipedia Mitsubishi_Outlander (IV, GN, 2021-). PHEV only in EU. 2.5 Atkinson + dual e-motor AWD, 20kWh plug-in, system 222kW/302PS. No ICE-only or diesel.
DE Wikipedia Mitsubishi_ASX (I, GA, 2010-2022). Long lifecycle. Facelift 2016, 2019. CVT = INVECS-III. 2.2 Di-D discontinued 2017.
DE Wikipedia Mitsubishi_ASX (II, 2023-). Rebadged Renault Captur II. CMP platform PSA. 1.6 Hybrid = E-Tech non-plug dog-clutch DHT → automatic. PHEV = E-Tech plug-in.

DE Wikipedia Mitsubishi_Eclipse_Cross (GK, 2017-). Facelift 2021: PHEV added, 1.5T petrol retained in some markets, SUV-coupé rear restyled to SUV. PHEV: 2.4 Atkinson + dual e-motor AWD. CVT = INVECS-III.

DE Wikipedia Mitsubishi_Pajero_II (V20/V40, 1991-1999). Body-on-frame 4WD. 6G72 V6 petrol, 4D56/4M40 diesel. Short/long wheelbase. SuperSelect 4WD. Popular used luxury off-roader import in PL 1995-2005.
DE Wikipedia Alfa_Romeo_159 (939, 2005-2011). GM Epsilon platform. Facelift 2008: 1.8 TBi replaces 2.2 JTS at base; 2.0 JTDM replaces 1.9. 3.2 V6 Q4 AWD. 2.4 JTDM 5-cyl. Selespeed on some petrol.
DE Wikipedia Alfa_Romeo_Giulia (952, 2016-). Giorgio platform. 2.0T GME engine (FWD or Q4 AWD). 280PS = Q4. 2.9 V6 Biturbo (Ferrari-derived) Quadrifoglio AWD. DSG = 8AT ZF.

DE Wikipedia Alfa_Romeo_Stelvio (949, 2017-). Giorgio platform, SUV sibling of Giulia. All variants AWD. 2.0T base = RWD or Q4 depending on market; EU often Q4 only. 2.9 V6 = Quadrifoglio. DSG = 8AT ZF.
DE Wikipedia Alfa_Romeo_Tonale (965, 2022-). STLA Small platform. 1.5 VGT = 48V mHEV with variable-geometry turbo, 7DCT → DSG → PB_95. PHEV: 1.3T + 90kW rear e-motor (Q4 AWD); 13.2kWh battery.

DE Wikipedia Alfa_Romeo_Junior (1.4/Milano, 2024-). CMP platform (Stellantis). Rebadged Peugeot e-208 platform. 1.2T PureTech + 48V BSG → PB_95. EV: 54kWh/115kW. Veloce PHEV: 1.2T + rear e-motor AWD, system 206kW.

DE Wikipedia Porsche_911 (991, 2011-2019). 991.1 (2011-2015): NA 3.4/3.8. 991.2 (2015-2019): turbocharged 3.0. DSG = 7-speed PDK. GT3 991.1: 3.8 475PS NA; 991.2: 4.0 500PS NA. Carrera AWD variants (Carrera 4) have same power.
DE Wikipedia Porsche_911 (992, 2019-). Turbo: 3.7L biturbo. Carrera 4/4S/Targa = same power as RWD. DSG = 8-speed PDK. GT3 available 6MT or PDK.
DE Wikipedia Porsche_Cayenne (958, 2010-2018). 958.1 (2010-2014): 4.8 V8 S/Turbo. 958.2 facelift (2014-2018): new 3.6T V6 S/GTS, PHEV. S E-Hybrid: 3.0 V6 + 95kW e-motor, 10.8kWh. DSG = 8AT Tiptronic.
DE Wikipedia Porsche_Cayenne (9YA, 2018-). MLB Evo platform. E-Hybrid PHEVs: 14.1kWh (2018-), 25.9kWh (2023 facelift). Turbo E-Hybrid: 4.0 V8 + 190kW e-motor, 739PS system. DSG = 8AT Tiptronic.
DE Wikipedia Porsche_Macan (95B, 2014-2024). MLB Evo platform (Audi Q5 based). 2.0T base = Audi EA888. Facelift 2018: new 2.9/3.0 V6 range. S Diesel 3.0 discontinued 2019. DSG = 7-speed PDK.
DE Wikipedia Porsche_Macan (J1A/EV, 2024-). PPE platform (shared with Audi Q6 e-tron). BEV only. Macan 4: 100kWh/single motor. 4S: 100kWh/dual motor AWD. Turbo: 100kWh/dual motor high-output.
DE Wikipedia Porsche_Boxster (981, 2012-2016). Pre-718 generation. NA flat-6. DSG = 7-speed PDK.
DE Wikipedia Porsche_718_Boxster (982, 2016-). 2.0T flat-4 turbo (base/S) from launch. GTS and Spyder: NA 4.0 flat-6. DSG = 7-speed PDK.
DE Wikipedia Porsche_Cayman (981c, 2012-2016). Coupe sibling of Boxster 981. NA flat-6. DSG = 7-speed PDK.
DE Wikipedia Porsche_718_Cayman (982c, 2016-). Coupe sibling of 718 Boxster. GTS/GT4/GT4 RS all use NA 4.0 flat-6. GT4 RS: 911 GT3 engine, PDK only. DSG = 7-speed PDK.

DE Wikipedia Saab_9-3 (YS3F, 2002-2012). GM Epsilon platform. 2.0 B207 family (1.8t/2.0t/2.0T). 2.8T V6 B284 XWD = AWD. 1.9 TiD = Fiat Multijet (ALFA). TTiD = BiTurbo 180PS. Production ended with Saab bankruptcy 2012.

DE Wikipedia Saab_9-5 (II/YS3G, 2010-2012). GM Epsilon II platform. Very short production (2yr) due to Saab bankruptcy. XWD = AWD. 2.8T V6 = Opel/Vauxhall B284. 2.0 TTiD BiTurbo. Transmission: 6MT or Aisin 6AT.
DE Wikipedia Jeep_Grand_Cherokee (WK2, 2010-2021). 8AT ZF. 3.6 Pentastar from 2011. SRT trim 6.4 HEMI. 3.0 EcoDiesel (VM Motori) for EU. All AWD standard.
DE Wikipedia Jeep_Grand_Cherokee (WL, 2021-). Giorgio platform hybrid (Stellantis + FCA). 4xe: 2.0T + e-motor AWD, 17.3kWh plug-in. 8AT. 3.0 EcoDiesel not available in WL EU.
DE Wikipedia Jeep_Wrangler (JK, 2007-2018). 2-door and 4-door Unlimited. EU market: 2.8 CRDi (VM Motori). 3.6 Pentastar added 2012. Manual 6MT or 5AT.
DE Wikipedia Jeep_Wrangler (JL, 2018-). 4xe: 2.0T + e-motor, 17.3kWh plug-in. 8AT (4xe/EcoDiesel); 6MT option on V6. 3.0 EcoDiesel EU primary diesel.
DE Wikipedia Jeep_Compass (MP, 2017-). Small Compass, EU build (Melfi, Italy). 1.3T = Stellantis GSE turbo. 4xe: 1.3T front + 60kW rear e-motor, 11.4kWh plug-in AWD. DSG = 7DCT.
DE Wikipedia Dodge_Charger (LD, 2011-2023). RWD or AWD. 8AT (ZF or TorqueFlite). V8 variants widely gray-imported to Poland. Hellcat: supercharged 6.2. Redeye: higher-output Hellcat.
DE Wikipedia Dodge_Challenger (LC, 2008-2023). RWD coupe. Manual available on V6/5.7/6.4. Hellcat/Redeye: automatic only (TorqueFlite 8AT).
DE Wikipedia Dodge_Grand_Caravan (RT, 2008-2020). US-market sibling of Chrysler Grand Voyager. No diesel. Gray imports to PL/EU. 6AT. Sold as bare-bones SE alongside premium Chrysler Town & Country.
DE Wikipedia Dodge_Durango (DS, 2011-). 3-row SUV, AWD or RWD. Platform shared with Jeep Grand Cherokee WK2. 8AT TorqueFlite. Hellcat edition: limited 2021 production only. No diesel in EU spec.
DE Wikipedia Chevrolet_Camaro (Gen6, 2016-2024). Alpha platform (Cadillac CT6). RWD. Limited official EU presence; gray imports common in PL. 10AT or 8AT. Production ended 2024.
DE Wikipedia Chevrolet_Corvette (C7, 2014-2019). Front-mid V8 RWD. LT1 (Stingray/Grand Sport), LS3 never. Z06/ZR1: supercharged LT4/LT5. 7MT or 8AT.

DE Wikipedia Chevrolet_Corvette (C8, 2020-). Mid-engine layout. LT2 V8 + 8DCT (dual clutch) → DSG. Z06: flat-plane 5.5 V8 LT6. E-Ray: 6.2 V8 + 97kW front e-motor, non-plug HEV → PB_95 (uses gen energy from engine). AWD on E-Ray.
DE Wikipedia Chevrolet_Suburban (K2UC/GMTK2, 2015-2020). Full-size body-on-frame SUV, 8-9 seats. LWB version of Tahoe. 6AT→8AT Hydra-Matic. 3.0 Duramax inline-6 added for MY2020 only.
DE Wikipedia Chevrolet_Suburban (T1XX, 2021-). New gen with independent rear suspension. 10AT Hydra-Matic. RWD or 4WD. Platform shared with Tahoe T1XX, GMC Yukon XL, Cadillac Escalade ESV.
DE Wikipedia Chevrolet_Tahoe (K2UC/GMTK2, 2015-2020). SWB version of Suburban. 5-door 8-seat SUV, 6AT→8AT. No diesel option (diesel only added on MY2020 Suburban, not Tahoe).
DE Wikipedia Chevrolet_Tahoe (T1XX, 2021-). New gen, independent rear suspension. 10AT Hydra-Matic. RWD or 4WD. Diesel LM2 Duramax added from launch (unlike K2UC).
DE Wikipedia Chevrolet_Aveo (T200, 2002-2006). Rebranded Daewoo Kalos. B-segment. Sold in PL as Chevrolet from 2004.
DE Wikipedia Chevrolet_Aveo (T250, 2006-2011). Facelift/new body. B-segment. Superseded by T300 in 2011.
DE Wikipedia Chevrolet_Lacetti (J200, 2003-2008). Successor to Daewoo Nubira. C-segment. Replaced by Cruze 2009. Very popular in PL mid-2000s.
DE Wikipedia Chevrolet_Captiva (C100, 2006-2015). Theta platform SUV, GM Daewoo origin. Popular in PL as affordable midsize SUV. Opel Antara is sister model.
DE Wikipedia RAM_1500 (DS Classic, 2009-2021, sold alongside DT). 8AT or 6AT depending on year. Gray imports popular in PL. 5.7 HEMI MDS cylinder deactivation. EcoDiesel: VM Motori sourced.
DE Wikipedia RAM_1500 (DT, 2018-). 5th gen. eTorque = 48V belt-starter-generator mHEV → PB_95. 8AT. TRX: supercharged Hellcat 6.2. Popular grey import to PL.
DE Wikipedia Chrysler_Voyager (RG/RS, 2001-2007). In EU sold as Voyager (SWB) and Grand Voyager (LWB). EU diesel: VM Motori sourced (2.5 CRD then 2.8 CRD). 3.8 V6 = Town & Country US equivalent. 4AT.
DE Wikipedia Chrysler_Grand_Voyager (RT, 2008-2016). US equivalent: Town & Country. EU had diesel 2.8 CRD (VM Motori) throughout; petrol switched 3.3→3.6 Pentastar 2011. 6AT Aisin.
DE Wikipedia Chrysler_Pacifica (RU, 2017-). Replaced Grand Voyager. Standard: 3.6 Pentastar + 9AT. Hybrid: 3.6L Atkinson + dual e-motors, 16→18.2kWh PHEV, e-CVT style → automatic. System 194kW/264PS.
DE Wikipedia Cadillac_Escalade (GMT900, 2007-2014). Full-size luxury BOF SUV. 6.2 V8 L92 403HP/409PS. 6AT Hydra-Matic. RWD or AWD. Also ESV (extended) variant. Premium gray import to PL.
DE Wikipedia Cadillac_Escalade (K2XX/GMTK2, 2015-2020). Shared platform with Suburban K2UC. 6.2 V8 L86 420HP/426PS. 8AT Hydra-Matic. AWD or RWD. ESV (long) and standard variants.
DE Wikipedia Cadillac_Escalade (T1XX, 2021-). New independent rear suspension. 10AT Hydra-Matic. 6.2 V8 L87 420HP. Diesel LM2. Escalade V: supercharged LT4 V8 682HP/692PS from 2023. Also ESV variant.

DE Wikipedia Lincoln_Navigator (L663, 2018-). Premium full-size BOF SUV on same Expedition platform. Twin-turbo 3.5 EB 450HP/456PS. 10AT. AWD or RWD. Standard and L (extended) wheelbase. Premium gray import to PL.

DE Wikipedia GMC_Yukon (T1XX, 2021-). Sibling of Chevrolet Suburban (Yukon XL) / Tahoe (Yukon). Same GMT T1XX platform and engines. 10AT Hydra-Matic. Denali and AT4 trims most common as EU gray imports.
DE Wikipedia Daewoo_Matiz (M150, 1998-2005). SOHC engines. Popular budget city car in PL in early 2000s. Successor Chevrolet Spark 2005+.
DE Wikipedia Daewoo_Lanos (T100, 1997-2004). Also assembled by FSO in Poland. Subcompact/compact. Sold in PL both as Daewoo and later FSO Lanos.
DE Wikipedia Daewoo_Nubira (J100/J150, 1997-2003). C-segment. Very common in PL 2000-2003. 2.0 CDX top trim. Replaced by Chevrolet Lacetti.
DE Wikipedia Daewoo_Tico (1991-2001). A-segment, based on Suzuki Alto. F8C engine. Assembled by FSO/Daewoo Poland in Lublin. Very popular in PL as budget first car in 1990s.
DE Wikipedia Honda_Civic_VII (EP1-EP4, 2001-2005). EU hatchback platform. 1.6 D16 VTEC, Type R K20A2 DOHC i-VTEC. 1.7 CDTi = Isuzu diesel. Popular as reliable used C-segment in PL.
DE Wikipedia Honda_Civic_VIII (FD/FN, 2005-2011). EU 5-door hatch = FN. R18A2 petrol, K20Z4 Type R, N22A diesel. Dual-zone dashboard controversial styling. Facelift 2008.
DE Wikipedia Honda_Jazz_GD (2001-2008). B-segment. L12A/L13A i-DSi (dual sequential ignition). 5-door only in EU. Excellent packaging with folding seats. Popular in PL as reliable city car.
DE Wikipedia Honda_Jazz_GE (2008-2015). 2nd gen EU. L12B/L13Z i-VTEC. Facelift 2011. Honda Magic Seats retained. Popular in PL as spacious B-segment.
DE Wikipedia Honda_Jazz_GK (2015-2020). 3rd gen EU. L13Z/L15B. CVT = Earth Dreams. Facelift 2018. Succeeded by GR e:HEV-only.
DE Wikipedia Honda_Jazz_GR (2020-). EU-only e:HEV full-hybrid. LEB (1.5 Atkinson) + 2 e-motors, e-CVT. Sold as Fit in other markets. Crosstar subvariant (raised ride height).
DE Wikipedia Honda_CR-V_II (RD4/RD5/RD7, 2001-2006). Compact SUV. K20A4 petrol. N22A diesel (from 2004). AWD real-time system. Popular in PL 2004-2008 as used.
DE Wikipedia Honda_CR-V_III (RE4/RE5, 2006-2012). Larger platform. K20Z4 petrol. N22A → N22B diesel. Facelift 2010. Real-time AWD. Popular in PL 2009-2014 as used compact SUV.
DE Wikipedia Honda_CR-V_IV (RM1/RM4, 2012-2018). EU: 1.6 i-DTEC diesel dominant. N16A diesel. R20A petrol + CVT. Facelift 2015 adds 160 KM diesel. 9AT later in US.
DE Wikipedia Honda_CR-V_V (RW1/RW2, 2018-2022). EU: 1.5T L15BE or 2.0 i-MMD (Atkinson + 2 e-motors, e-CVT). AWD = real-time e-AWD on hybrid. No diesel in Mk5.
DE Wikipedia Honda_CR-V_VI (2022-). EU: e:HEV full hybrid only (no ICE-only). PHEV = Sport Touring e:PHEV. 2.0 LEB Atkinson + dual e-motor system. e-CVT.
DE Wikipedia Honda_HR-V_II (RU1/RU6, 2015-2021). EU B/C-SUV. L15B2 petrol / N16A diesel. Facelift 2018. Real-time AWD optional. Popular in PL 2016-2022 as used compact SUV.
DE Wikipedia Honda_HR-V_III (RV3/RV5, 2021-). EU: e:HEV only (no diesel, no ICE-only). LEB Atkinson + 2 e-motors, e-CVT. FWD standard, eAWD optional.
DE Wikipedia Honda_Accord_VII (CM2/CM3, 2003-2008). EU D/E-segment. K20A6/K24A3 petrol, J30A4 V6, N22A diesel. Sedan + Tourer estate. Facelift 2006.
DE Wikipedia Honda_Accord_VIII (CU1/CU2, 2008-2015). EU D/E-segment. R20A3 / K24Z3 petrol, N22B diesel. Facelift 2011. Tourer estate. Last Accord sold in EU until 2023 relaunch.
DE Wikipedia Honda_Civic_IX (FK2/FK3, 2011-2017). EU hatch. R18Z4 / N16A. Type R FK2 = K20C2 turbo, 310 PS, FWD only. Facelift 2014.
DE Wikipedia Honda_Civic_X (FC1/FK7/FK8, 2017-2021). EU hatch 5-door. P10A3 1.0T / L15B7 1.5T. Type R FK8 = K20C1 turbo 320PS FWD. Facelift 2019. Last diesel in 2020.
DE Wikipedia Honda_Civic_XI (FL1/FL5, 2022-). EU: e:HEV full hybrid (LEB + 2 e-motors) as standard. PHEV also available. Type R FL5 = K20C1 329PS. No diesel offered.
DE Wikipedia Honda_Civic_V (EG, 1991-1995). C-segment. D13B2/D15Z1/D16Z6 VTEC. 3/4/5-door. First VTEC in a mainstream Honda. Popular reliability-first used import in PL 1995-2002.
DE Wikipedia Honda_Civic_VI (EK, 1995-2001). C-segment. D14A/D15Z/D16Y/B18C6 engines. Type R = B18C6 DOHC VTEC 8500 rpm. Cult used import in PL 1998-2005 for tuner community.
DE Wikipedia Suzuki_Jimny_III (FJ, 1998-2018). Compact off-roader, 4WD ladder frame. G13BB/M13A engine. One of the longest single-gen runs. Very popular in PL for off-road use.
DE Wikipedia Suzuki_Jimny_IV (GJ, 2018-). Body-on-frame 4WD retained. K15B engine. Sold as 2-seater commercial in EU from 2019 due to CO2 regs. Cult following.
DE Wikipedia Suzuki_Grand_Vitara_II (JT, 2005-2015). 3/5-door compact SUV. J20A petrol; 1.9 DDiS = Fiat 188A9.000 diesel. Shift-on-the-fly 4WD. Popular in PL as affordable 4WD SUV.
DE Wikipedia Suzuki_Vitara_III (LY, 2015-). Crossover. K16B (1.6), K14C BoosterJet turbo, K15C mHEV 48V. AllGrip AWD. Facelift 2018 + MHEV 2019. Sold well in PL.
DE Wikipedia Suzuki_Swift_II (RS, 2004-2010). B-segment. M13A/M15A petrol; 1.3 DDiS = Fiat 199A2 diesel. Sport 1.6 = M16A. Popular fun city car in PL.
DE Wikipedia Suzuki_Swift_III (FZ/NZ, 2010-2017). B-segment. K12B petrol; M16A Sport. 1.3 DDiS diesel. Facelift 2013. AWD optional. Popular in PL 2011-2017.
DE Wikipedia Suzuki_Swift_IV (AZ, 2017-). B-segment, lighter platform. K10C BoosterJet; K12D DualJet MHEV 48V; K14D Sport. Facelift 2020 drops 1.0T → DualJet MHEV standard.
DE Wikipedia Suzuki_SX4_I (EY, 2006-2014). Compact crossover/hatch. M15A/M16A petrol; 1.6 DDiS / 1.9 DDiS = Fiat diesel. AllGrip 4WD. Developed with Fiat (→ Fiat Sedici).
DE Wikipedia Suzuki_S-Cross_I (JY, 2013-2021). Compact crossover. M16A / K14C turbo / Fiat DDiS diesel. Facelift 2016 adds BoosterJet. AllGrip AWD. Sold in PL as mid-size alternative to Vitara.
DE Wikipedia Suzuki_S-Cross_II (AKK, 2021-). K14D MHEV 48V or 1.5T PHEV (Toyota sourced TNGA-based, 17.4 kWh). AllGrip AWD. No diesel. Facelift approach vs. all-new.
DE Wikipedia Suzuki_Baleno_II (FW, 2015-). B-segment hatch. K10C BoosterJet; K12C DualJet + SHVS mild hybrid. Facelift 2019 + 2022. FWD only. Affordable B-hatch in PL.
DE Wikipedia Suzuki_Ignis_III (FF21S, 2016-). Mini crossover. K12D DualJet + SHVS/MHEV 48V. AllGrip AWD optional. Retro design. City car with high driving position. Niche but visible in PL.
DE Wikipedia Subaru_Impreza_II (GD/GG, 2000-2007). EJ20 flat-4. WRX = EJ20T turbo 220PS; STI = EJ207 265PS with DCCD diff. EU spec. Sedan/wagon. Popular rally-image import in PL.
DE Wikipedia Subaru_Impreza_III (GH/GE, 2007-2011). EJ20 (2.0i) / EJ255 (WRX 2.5T) / EJ257 (STI 2.5T). EE20 boxer diesel added 2009. Switched to hatchback only in EU.
DE Wikipedia Subaru_Impreza_IV (GJ/GP, 2011-2016). FB16/FB20 new boxer platform. No WRX in EU (became separate model). CVT = Lineartronic. Hatch/sedan.
DE Wikipedia Subaru_Impreza_V (GT/GK, 2016-). SGP platform (new). FB16E/FB20E. e-Boxer = 12.3V mild hybrid assist motor. AWD Symmetrical standard. Hatch/sedan.
DE Wikipedia Subaru_Legacy_IV (BL/BP, 2003-2009). D-segment. EJ20/EJ25 boxer petrol; EZ30 flat-6 (3.0R); EE20 boxer diesel late. BP = Outback variant shared. AWD Symmetrical.
DE Wikipedia Subaru_Legacy_V (BM/BR, 2009-2014). EJ20/EJ25/EE20. BR = Outback. Lineartronic CVT introduced. Popular in PL as spacious AWD estate (BR/Outback more than BM sedan).
DE Wikipedia Subaru_Outback_III (BP, 2003-2009). Legacy BP estate with raised suspension + plastic cladding. EJ253/EJ30/EE20. AWD Symmetrical. Popular in PL as practical lifted estate.
DE Wikipedia Subaru_Outback_IV (BR, 2009-2014). Legacy V platform. EJ20/EJ25/EE20. Lineartronic CVT. AWD Symmetrical. Good seller in PL as used AWD estate alternative.
DE Wikipedia Subaru_Outback_V (BS, 2014-2019). FB25 petrol, EE20 diesel, EZ36 V6 (US/AU mainly). 3.6R limited EU availability — medium confidence. Lineartronic CVT.
DE Wikipedia Subaru_Outback_VI (BT, 2019-). SGP platform. FB25 standard or + e-Boxer mild hybrid motor. No diesel in Mk6. AWD Symmetrical. Eyesight standard.
DE Wikipedia Subaru_Forester_II (SG, 2002-2008). EJ20/EJ20T boxer. Compact SUV, high ground clearance. AWD Symmetrical. No diesel in Mk2. Facelift 2005. Popular in PL as practical AWD.
DE Wikipedia Subaru_Forester_III (SH, 2008-2013). EJ20/EJ205 petrol, EE20 boxer diesel. EyeSight ADAS introduced. Facelift 2011. Popular in PL especially diesel AWD variant.
DE Wikipedia Subaru_Forester_IV (SJ, 2013-2018). FB20/FA20DIT turbo, EE20 diesel. Lineartronic CVT. Facelift 2016. AWD Symmetrical. Popular in PL 2014-2020 as used.
DE Wikipedia Subaru_Forester_V (SK, 2018-). SGP platform. FB20E e-Boxer MHEV (EU main seller) or FB25 (US/AU). No diesel, no turbo in EU. EyeSight standard. AWD Symmetrical.
DE Wikipedia Subaru_XV_I (GP, 2012-2017). Impreza GP raised crossover. FB16/FB20/EE20. AWD Symmetrical. Popular in PL as affordable compact AWD crossover vs. Forester.
DE Wikipedia Subaru_XV_II (GT, 2017-2023). SGP platform. FB16/FB20E + e-Boxer. PHEV = Plug-in Hybrid XV. No diesel. AWD Symmetrical. Sold as Crosstrek in non-EU markets.
DE Wikipedia Subaru_BRZ_I (ZC6, 2012-2021). FR sports coupe, co-developed with Toyota (GT86/GR86). FA20D boxer 4-cyl, D4-S injection. RWD. Torsen LSD available. Cult following in PL.
DE Wikipedia Subaru_BRZ_II (ZD8, 2021-). Co-developed with Toyota (GR86 II). FA24D naturally aspirated boxer, larger displacement for better torque. RWD only. Traction control / LSD.

DE Wikipedia Fiat_Polonez / FSO_Polonez (1978-2002). Large family car, Poland-designed on Fiat 125p platform. Later = Caro/Atu variants. 1.5 MPI = Fiat Fire injection. 1.6D = VM Motori diesel. Iconic PL domestic car.
DE Wikipedia Citroën_Saxo (S0, 1996-2004). B-segment, Peugeot 106 sibling. TU series. VTS = sport trim 16v. Shared with 106. Popular budget sporty B-segment in PL 1998-2004.
